input.initialPrompt: Modernize this project to SDK-style format. Identify all legacy patterns and suggest how to simplify the project file.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo and Properties dir

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo and Properties dir

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a full picture. Let me identify the legacy patterns and modernize this project.

Legacy patterns found:
1. `ToolsVersion="15.0"` and `xmlns` attribute — SDK-style uses `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">`
2. Explicit `<Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\...Microsoft.Common.props" />` — SDK handles this
3. Explicit `<Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />` — SDK handles this
4. Boilerplate PropertyGroup: Configuration, Platform defaults, ProjectGuid, FileAlignment — all SDK defaults


5. Debug/Release conditional PropertyGroups with OutputPath, DebugSymbols, DebugType, Optimize, DefineConstants, ErrorReport, WarningLevel — SDK provides sensible defaults for these
6. System references and other framework assemblies listed explicitly — SDK-style projects reference them implicitly
7. Every source file enumerated with `<Compile Include>` — SDK uses automatic file globbing instead
8. Separate `Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs` file — SDK auto-generates assembly attributes from project properties
9. `TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2` needs to become `TargetFramework>net472`
10. `GenerateBindingRedirects` and `Deterministic` are already SDK defaults

The modernized version will be significantly leaner. I should also remove the AssemblyInfo.cs file since the SDK will generate those attributes automatically from the project file settings.

Now I'll convert it.

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <AssemblyTitle>LegacyApp</AssemblyTitle>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Product>LegacyApp</Product>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

toolArgs.description: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo and Properties dir

data.content:
Done. Here's what was modernized:

**Legacy patterns removed:**

| Pattern | Why it's unnecessary |
|---------|---------------------|
| `ToolsVersion`, `xmlns`, XML declaration | Replaced by `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"` |
| Explicit `Import` of Common.props / CSharp.targets | SDK handles this automatically |
| `Configuration`, `Platform`, `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment` | SDK defaults |
| Debug/Release conditional PropertyGroups | SDK provides sensible defaults for both |
| `OutputType=Library` | SDK default |
| `RootNamespace`/`AssemblyName` matching project name | Inferred from filename |
| `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`, `Deterministic` | SDK defaults |
| Explicit `<Compile Include="..."/>` items | SDK uses implicit globbing |
| `<Reference Include="System"/>` etc. | Implicit for .NET Framework in SDK-style |
| `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Attributes auto-generated from `<PropertyGroup>` |

**Result — 11 lines vs 47:**

```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <AssemblyTitle>LegacyApp</AssemblyTitle>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Product>LegacyApp</Product>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```
